text: 6 Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 6分钟英语,来自bbclearningenglish.com

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好,欢迎收听BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Pippa. Whatever you think of social media, in less than a generation it has changed the internet.
zh: 我是Neil。我是Pippa。不管你怎么看待社交媒体,不到一代人的时间里,它已经改变了互联网。

text: Facebook, Twitter and in the early days,
zh: Facebook、Twitter,还有早期的

text: Myspace used to be places to post messages to friends or share photos of your breakfast.
zh: Myspace,曾经是人们发消息给朋友或分享早餐照片的地方。

text: But with the rise of AI, the fun of connecting with friends
zh: 但随着人工智能的兴起,与朋友联系的乐趣

text: changed into something much stranger, as Tristan and Asma, presenters of BBC World Service programme The Global Story, found
zh: 变成了更奇怪的东西,这是BBC世界服务节目《The Global Story》的主持人Tristan和Asma发现的。

text: out. Oh, what is this? I think it's a man who's supposed to look like
zh: 哦,这是什么?我想这是一个被打扮成

text: Jesus. It's a man who looks a bit like... Who also looks like his dog.
zh: 耶稣样子的男人。这个男人看起来有点像……他也长得像他的狗。

text: He's been AI'd, right? Has to be AI'd. He's holding his dog and his face looks like his dog, so he's half Jesus, half dog.
zh: 这是AI生成的吧?肯定是AI做的。他抱着他的狗,脸却长得像狗,所以他是一半耶稣一半狗。

text: A half dog, half Jesus photo is generated by AI and posted online.
zh: 这张一半是狗、一半是耶稣的照片是由AI生成并发布到网上的。

text: It's an example of how the use of AI in social media has evolved
zh: 这正说明了AI在社交媒体中的应用已经发展

text: so much that today it's hard to know what's created by humans and what's not.
zh: 到了如此地步,如今很难分辨什么是人创作的,什么不是。

text: In this episode, we'll discuss how social media is changing in the age of AI.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将讨论在AI时代,社交媒体正在如何变化。

text: And as usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 和往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary, along with a transcript, on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇和文字稿。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Pippa. You mentioned MySpace as one of the earlier social media platforms,
zh: 不过,首先我有个问题要问Pippa。你提到MySpace是早期的社交媒体平台之一,

text: but the first electronic message was actually sent much earlier, in 1844,
zh: 但其实第一条电子讯息发得更早,是在1844年,

text: using a series of dots and dashes tapped out by hand on a telegraph wire.
zh: 是靠手动敲击电报线上的一串点和划发送的。

text: So what was this new form of communication called?
zh: 那么,这种新的通讯方式叫什么呢?

text: Was it A, semaphore, B, dial -up, or C, Morse code?
zh: 是A、旗语,B、拨号上网,还是C、摩斯电码?

text: I think Morse code. OK, we'll find out the answer later.
zh: 我觉得是摩斯电码。好,我们稍后揭晓答案。

text: The introduction of AI has turned some users away from social media.
zh: AI的引入让一些用户开始远离社交媒体。

text: Indeed, a recent report found that social media activity peaked in 2022 and has been going down ever since.
zh: 确实,最近的一份报告发现,社交媒体的活跃度在2022年达到顶峰,之后就一直在下降。

text: Millennial and digital native Kyle Chaker is a writer specialising in social media.
zh: 千禧一代、数字原住民Kyle Chaker是一位专注社交媒体的作家。

text: He spoke about the problem to Tristan Redmond for BBC World Service programme The Global Story.
zh: 他就这个问题接受了BBC世界服务节目《The Global Story》Tristan Redmond的采访。

text: OK, Kyle, was there a moment for you when you felt like social media changed?
zh: 好的,Kyle,对你来说,有没有那么一个时刻,让你感觉社交媒体变了?

text: Oh man, I mean, in 2015, 2016,
zh: 哦天哪,我记得是2015、2016年左右,

text: I think, in the United States, when Twitter became the hub of election discourse,
zh: 在美国,当Twitter成为大选话题讨论的中心时,

text: it kind of shattered the illusion that the internet was a space you could go to just like, have fun and express yourself and
zh: 它打破了一种幻觉,那种认为互联网只是一个可以尽情玩乐、表达自我、

text: find cool friends and discuss the latest cultural happenings.
zh: 结交有趣朋友、讨论最新文化热点的空间。

text: Kyle uses the informal expression, oh man! An exclamation showing excitement or
zh: Kyle用了一个口语表达 oh man! 用来表示激动或

text: enthusiasm. He remembers the internet in 2016 when Twitter was full of angry political debate about the US elections.
zh: 热情。他回忆起2016年的互联网,当时Twitter上充斥着关于美国大选的愤怒政治争论。

text: For Kyle, this shattered the illusion that the internet was
zh: 对Kyle来说,这打破了互联网

text: fun. If something shatters your illusions, it destroys a false belief you hold, making you realise an unpleasant truth.
zh: 有趣的幻觉。如果某事shatter你的幻觉,就是摧毁了你原本的错误认知,让你意识到一个令人不快的事实。

text: Kyle thinks social media was friendlier before –
zh: Kyle认为社交媒体以前更友好,

text: an online space to make friends, have fun and discuss the latest cultural happenings.
zh: 是一个可以交朋友、找乐子、讨论最新文化事件的网络空间。

text: A cultural happening is an event that embodies the values and interests of a particular group at a particular time in history.
zh: cultural happening(文化盛事)指的是某个特定历史时期,体现某个群体价值观和兴趣的事件。

text: For example, the hit television show Game of Thrones was a cultural happening for millennials in the 2010s.
zh: 比如,热门剧集《权力的游戏》就是2010年代千禧一代的一个文化盛事。

text: So, what's the future of social media? Will the amount of AI -generated content continue to grow.
zh: 那么,社交媒体的未来会是怎样?AI生成内容的数量会持续增长吗?

text: Here's Kyle Chaker again sharing his thoughts with BBC World Service's The Global Story.
zh: 再次请来Kyle Chaker,与BBC世界服务《The Global Story》分享他的看法。

text: We see the large social networks and the tech behemoths leaning into generated content,
zh: 我们看到大型社交网络和科技巨头都在倾向于生成式内容,

text: and I think users don't actually want that. I think it's a novelty for now and people will drift away from it.
zh: 但我认为用户其实并不想要这些。我觉得这只是暂时的新奇感,人们最终会渐渐远离它。

text: So instead I see people gravitating towards smaller online spaces,
zh: 所以我反而看到人们正转向更小的网络空间,

text: connecting more more closely with individual voices like creator types and just posting less in general.
zh: 更紧密地与像创作者这样的个体声音互动,总体上发帖也更少了。

text: Kyle thinks the tech behemoths, meaning large and powerful companies, will continue pushing AI.
zh: Kyle认为这些科技巨头,也就是那些庞大而强势的公司,会继续推动AI的发展。

text: However, many people want content which is more personal and meaningful.
zh: 然而,许多人想要的是更个性化、更有意义的内容。

text: They think AI is a novelty, something which is new and unusual and therefore exciting, but which probably won't last.
zh: 他们认为AI只是一种novelty(新奇事物),新颖有趣,但可能不会持久。

text: Instead, Kyle thinks people will gravitate towards smaller online
zh: 相反,Kyle认为人们会更倾向于

text: communities. If you gravitate towards something, you are naturally drawn or attracted to it.
zh: 小型社群。如果你gravitate towards(被……吸引)某事物,就是自然而然地被它吸引。

text: Social media has changed fast and is still in
zh: 社交媒体变化很快,而且仍在

text: motion. It's come a long way from the first electronic messages of 1844, which you mentioned in your question, Neil.
zh: 不断演变。相比你在问题里提到的1844年第一条电子讯息,它已经走了很长一段路,Neil。

text: So, did I get the correct answer? I asked what this new form of communication was,
zh: 那么,我答对了吗?我问的是这种新型通讯方式叫什么,

text: and you said that it was C, Morse code, Ode and that, Pippa, is the correct answer.
zh: 你说是C,摩斯电码,Pippa,这就是正确答案。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with the exclamation, oh man, used to show excitement or enthusiasm.
zh: 好,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是感叹词oh man,用来表示激动或热情。

text: To shatter an illusion is to destroy someone's misconceptions, causing them to realise an unpleasant reality.
zh: shatter an illusion(打破幻觉)指摧毁某人原本的错误认知,让他们意识到令人不快的现实。

text: A cultural happening is an event that embodies the values and interests of a particular social group at a particular time.
zh: cultural happening(文化盛事)指某一特定历史时期,体现某个社会群体价值观和兴趣的事件。

text: A behemoth is an extremely large and powerful company or organisation.
zh: behemoth(巨兽/巨头)指规模庞大、势力强大的公司或组织。

text: A novelty is something which is new and unusual and therefore exciting.
zh: novelty(新奇事物)指新颖、不寻常因而令人兴奋的东西。

text: And finally, if you gravitate towards something, you are naturally attracted to it.
zh: 最后,如果你gravitate towards(倾向、被吸引)某事物,就是自然而然被它吸引。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you're still using the internet,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过如果你还在上网,

text: head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com, where you'll find a worksheet and quiz for this episode.
zh: 欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,那里有本期的练习题和小测验。

text: See you again soon, but for now it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在先说再见。
